Tensor.name: shape
Tensor.description: Return the shape (size-per-dimension array) of the tensor.

Tensor.name: dtype
Tensor.description: Return the dtype string of the tensor.

Tensor.name: reshape
Tensor.description: Return a view with the given shape; total element count must match.

Tensor.name: permute
Tensor.description: Reorder all dimensions per the permutation list.

Tensor.name: expand
Tensor.description: Broadcast to a larger shape without copying memory.

Tensor.name: slice
Tensor.description: View a contiguous slice along the given dimension.

Tensor.name: unsqueeze
Tensor.description: Insert a size-1 dimension at the given position.

Tensor.name: squeeze
Tensor.description: Remove a size-1 dimension at the given position.

Tensor.name: select
Tensor.description: Select a single index along `dim`, removing that dimension.

Tensor.name: contiguous
Tensor.description: Return a row-major contiguous copy of the tensor.

Tensor.name: detach
Tensor.description: Return a copy detached from the autograd graph.

Tensor.name: backward
Tensor.description: Propagate gradients backward from this tensor.

Tensor.name: requires_grad
Tensor.description: Enable or disable gradient tracking on this tensor.

Tensor.name: grad
Tensor.description: Read the accumulated gradient of this leaf tensor.

Tensor.name: clone
Tensor.description: Return a deep copy of the tensor (separate storage).

Tensor.name: eq
Tensor.description: Element-wise equality comparison. Returns a boolean tensor.

Tensor.name: matmul
Tensor.description: Matrix multiplication; broadcasts on leading batch dimensions.

Tensor.name: sum
Tensor.description: Sum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: mean
Tensor.description: Arithmetic mean over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: max
Tensor.description: Maximum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: min
Tensor.description: Minimum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: argmax
Tensor.description: Index of the maximum along `axis`; `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: argmin
Tensor.description: Index of the minimum along `axis`; `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: prod
Tensor.description: Product of elements over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: softmax
Tensor.description: Softmax along `axis`, normalizing to a probability distribution.

Tensor.name: log_softmax
Tensor.description: Logarithm of softmax along `axis`, numerically stable.

Model.name: parameters
Model.description: Return the model's learnable parameter tensors.

Model.name: forward
Model.description: Run the model's forward block. Calling the model directly is equivalent.

Model.name: to
Model.description: Move the model's parameters to a device (`"cpu"`, `"gpu"`, `"webgpu"`) and return it.

Model.name: state_dict
Model.description: Return a serializable dict of parameter tensors.

Model.name: load_state_dict
Model.description: Load parameter tensors from a previously saved dict.

Model.name: save
Model.description: Save the model's weights to `path` (compact binary checkpoint). Reload into a same-architecture model with `load_model(model, path)`.

DataFrame.name: columns
DataFrame.description: Return the column names as an array of strings.

DataFrame.name: schema
DataFrame.description: Return the frame's schema (fields with names and data types).

DataFrame.name: explain
DataFrame.description: Return the logical query plan as a human-readable string.

DataFrame.name: select
DataFrame.description: Project a new frame from the given columns or `Column` expressions.

DataFrame.name: filter
DataFrame.description: Keep only rows matching a boolean `Column` (or SQL string) condition.

DataFrame.name: where
DataFrame.description: Alias for `filter`.

DataFrame.name: withColumn
DataFrame.description: Return a new frame with an added or replaced column computed from `column`.

DataFrame.name: drop
DataFrame.description: Return a new frame without the named columns.

DataFrame.name: groupBy
DataFrame.description: Group rows by the given columns, returning a `GroupedData` for aggregation.

DataFrame.name: orderBy
DataFrame.description: Sort rows. Each spec is a column name/`Column`, or `{ col, desc }` for ordering.

DataFrame.name: sort
DataFrame.description: Alias for `orderBy`.

DataFrame.name: limit
DataFrame.description: Return at most `count` rows, skipping the first `offset` rows.

DataFrame.name: head
DataFrame.description: Return the first `n` rows as a new frame (pandas-style preview).

DataFrame.name: distinct
DataFrame.description: Return a frame with duplicate rows removed.

DataFrame.name: union
DataFrame.description: Concatenate the rows of another frame with matching column types.

DataFrame.name: unionAll
DataFrame.description: Concatenate rows of another frame, keeping duplicates.

DataFrame.name: join
DataFrame.description:
Join with another frame on one or more key columns. `how` is one of
`INNER`, `LEFT`, `RIGHT`, or `FULL`.

DataFrame.name: collect
DataFrame.description: Execute the plan and return all rows as an array of objects.

DataFrame.name: toArray
DataFrame.description: Alias for `collect`.

DataFrame.name: count
DataFrame.description: Execute the plan and return the number of rows.

DataFrame.name: show
DataFrame.description: Execute and print the first `n` rows as a formatted table; returns the text.

DataFrame.name: chunks
DataFrame.description: Execute and stream results as an async iterator of data chunks.

DataFrame.name: to_tensor
DataFrame.description:
Materialize the (optionally selected) numeric columns into a 2-D tensor of
shape `[rows, columns]`. Non-numeric columns raise — encode them first.

DataFrame.name: to_array
DataFrame.description: Alias for `collect` — execute and return rows as an array of objects.

DataFrame.name: encode
DataFrame.description:
Encode a categorical column to integer ids, returning `[encoded_tensor,
classes_array]`. Pass `classes=` to reuse ids fitted on another frame.

GroupedData.name: agg
GroupedData.description:
Apply aggregate `Column` expressions (e.g. `sum`, `avg`, `count`) over each
group, returning a `DataFrame` of group keys and aggregates.

Column.name: alias
Column.description: Rename the column's output to `name`.

Column.name: as
Column.description: Alias for `alias`.

Column.name: add
Column.description: Arithmetic addition with another column or value.

Column.name: sub
Column.description: Arithmetic subtraction with another column or value.

Column.name: mul
Column.description: Arithmetic multiplication with another column or value.

Column.name: div
Column.description: Arithmetic division with another column or value.

Column.name: eq
Column.description: Equality comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: ne
Column.description: Inequality comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: lt
Column.description: Less-than comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: le
Column.description: Less-than-or-equal comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: gt
Column.description: Greater-than comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: ge
Column.description: Greater-than-or-equal comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: and
Column.description: Logical AND of two boolean columns.

Column.name: or
Column.description: Logical OR of two boolean columns.

Column.name: not
Column.description: Logical negation of a boolean column.

Column.name: isNull
Column.description: True where the column value is null.

Column.name: isNotNull
Column.description: True where the column value is not null.

Column.name: like
Column.description: SQL `LIKE` match against a string pattern.

Column.name: between
Column.description: True where the value lies in the inclusive range `[low, high]`.

Column.name: isin
Column.description: True where the value is one of the given values.

Column.name: cast
Column.description: Cast the column to another data type.

List.name: append
List.description: Add `x` to the end of the list, growing it in place.

List.name: extend
List.description: Append every element of list `other` to the end of this list, in place.

List.name: insert
List.description:
Insert `x` at index `i`, shifting later elements right. Negative `i` counts from
the end; out-of-range `i` is clamped to the nearest end.

List.name: pop
List.description:
Remove and return the element at index `i` (the last element when `i` is omitted).
Errors on an empty list.

List.name: remove
List.description: Remove the first element equal to `x`. Does nothing if no element matches.

List.name: index
List.description: Return the position of the first element equal to `x`, or `-1` if absent.

List.name: count
List.description: Return how many elements are equal to `x`.

List.name: contains
List.description: True when the list holds an element equal to `x`.

List.name: reverse
List.description: Reverse the order of the elements in place.

List.name: clear
List.description: Remove all elements, leaving an empty list.

List.name: copy
List.description: Return a shallow copy of the list.

String.name: upper
String.description: Return the string with every character upper-cased.

String.name: lower
String.description: Return the string with every character lower-cased.

String.name: strip
String.description: Return the string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

String.name: lstrip
String.description: Return the string with leading whitespace removed.

String.name: rstrip
String.description: Return the string with trailing whitespace removed.

String.name: split
String.description:
Split the string on `sep`. With no `sep`, split on runs of whitespace and drop
empty pieces.

String.name: join
String.description:
Join `parts` into one string using this string as the separator, e.g.
`", ".join(words)`.

String.name: replace
String.description: Return a copy with every occurrence of `old` replaced by `new`.

String.name: starts_with
String.description: True when the string begins with `prefix`.

String.name: ends_with
String.description: True when the string ends with `suffix`.

String.name: find
String.description: Return the index of the first occurrence of `sub`, or `-1` if absent.

String.name: contains
String.description: True when `sub` occurs anywhere in the string.

Dict.name: keys
Dict.description: Return a list of the dictionary's keys.

Dict.name: values
Dict.description: Return a list of the dictionary's values.

Dict.name: items
Dict.description: Return a list of `[key, value]` pairs.

Dict.name: get
Dict.description: Return the value for `key`, or `default` (or `null` when omitted) if the key is absent.

Dict.name: has
Dict.description: True when `key` is present in the dictionary.

Dict.name: remove
Dict.description: Remove `key` and its value from the dictionary. Does nothing if absent.

signature.display: arange(start: number, end?: number, step?: number) -> Tensor
linspace(start: number, end: number, steps: number) -> Tensor
where(condition: any, x: any, y: any) -> Tensor
range(start?: number, stop?: number, step: number = 1) -> Array

builtins.name: chart
builtins.description: Chart namespace for notebook visual outputs.

signature.display: line(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
bar(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
scatter(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
histogram(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
area(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
box(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
violin(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
density(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
correlation(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
hexbin(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
heatmap(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
regression(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
ecdf(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
bubble(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
funnel(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
waterfall(data: any, x?: number, y?: number, bins?: number, title?: string, x_label?: string, y_label?: string, ...options: any) -> ChartSpec
